module_mood_contract: concrete awe — the room sees agentic work as files, rules, prompts, evals, outputs, and versioned customer builds; leaves thinking 'this is not magic; it is structure'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands — every teaching beat 8+/10; 7 = facilitator-premium flag; <7 = REVISE.
rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[5] (Inspect the system) mood_score: 7. All other teaching beats 8–10. No beat below 7. Step 5 is facilitator-premium: 'the point is the map' is stated in the script body for the trainer but no spoken framing line is given — a trainer who stays silent watches a file enumeration, not a map reveal. Step 5 rises to 8 with one named trainer line: 'everything this workbook needs — source, render, output, deployment — lives in the same system.'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a parenthetical trainer line at Step 5: e.g. '(Say aloud: "Everything the workbook needs — source, render, output, deployment — lives right here.")' so the map frame is given to the room, not just held in the trainer's head.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M1 concrete awe must not steal M2's expanded-horizon teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Step 11 closes on 'That loop is the difference between asking Claude for help and working inside an agentic system.' This crystallises the M1 concrete-awe arc without surfacing the M2 forward-hunger framing ('where is this all going?'). The M2 bridge is correctly owned by the module wrapper (agentic-systems-repo-demo.md ## Handoff), not by this lecture script.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — the named teaching moment reliably triggers across reasonable persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc teaching moment: 'Claude is not only the chat box. The system is the set of files, rules, prompts, checks, and outputs that let work repeat and improve.' This fires reliably at Step 4 (trace.phases[4]): trainer asks why the workbook uses a specific phrase; Claude Code answers by citing CLAUDE.md; the room sees the chain rules → output with no room for the trainer to skip it. The Step 4 scaffolding gap (specific phrase not pre-highlighted in Step 2) is trainer-recoverable without losing the teaching moment. Crystallisation at Step 11 is fragile (no spoken line given), but Step 11 is the recognition close, not the primary teaching moment.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — the module's Big Idea (from strategy doc) is honored.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc Big Idea: 'Agentic systems are visible when you know where to look: rules, prompts, memory, evals, build scripts, customer versions, and commits.' The 11-step arc covers every named element: rules (Step 3), rules-steer-behavior (Step 4), system map including source/build/output/deploy (Step 5), source edit (Step 6), build output (Step 7), checks (Step 8), diff as receipt (Step 9), compounding as memory-of-rules (Step 10). The demo begins with the deployed workbook (Step 2) and closes by naming the loop (Step 11). No element of the Big Idea is missing from the arc.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — this file's close hands off to M2's open hook.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is a lecture/demo script; the bridge is owned by the module wrapper (agentic-systems-repo-demo.md ## Handoff: 'Now that you have seen the shape of a system, run one together: individual signal first, group synthesis second, cross-pollination only after synthesis.'). The script correctly closes on the teaching-moment crystallisation at Step 11 without pre-empting M2. Arc integrity is intact at the module level.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text — no authored conversations or pause cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'share with your neighbor,' 'discuss with...,' 'tell the room,' or trainer-cue beats in the script body. Steps are all trainer actions against the system or explicit questions the trainer asks Claude Code. Cohort management is absent from the text throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is a trainer demo script with no fenced prompt blocks. Rule 16 applies to student-facing exercises with copyable prompts. The script is the trainer's action spine; forcing-function mechanics are not the applicable construct here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lecture placement — demo lectures showing a ceiling go at the beginning; meta-frame lectures are closers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is a demo lecture showing a ceiling: what a real agentic system looks like, end to end. Per check_lectures rule 1, 'Demo lectures showing a ceiling go at the beginning.' It is positioned as the M1 lecture before M2's crux exercise. Correct placement.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises — pre-exercise lectures arm, not over-teach.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The demo is concrete and action-driven: 11 steps of showing the real system in operation. It does not lecture on agent architecture, taxonomy, or framework vocabulary before the M2 crux exercise. The demo arms the room with a mental model ('the loop') and then hands off. No over-teaching or framework-mapping before the exercise. Strategy doc confirms: 'The live lecture earns its place by showing a real repo, not by explaining abstractions.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function phase names its dominant failure and one recovery move.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Demo script has no fenced-prompt forcing functions. Maintainer block is minimal (draft quality tag only). This rule fires on exercises; not applicable to a trainer demo script.

stealing_the_mood: Step 5 (Inspect the system) — 7/10. 'The point is the map' is a teaching frame in the script body but no spoken narration line is given to the trainer. Without the frame spoken aloud, the room experiences a file enumeration, not a map reveal. Facilitator-premium gap: a skilled trainer takes this to 8 easily; the script leaves the room dependent on trainer instinct.

todos.description: Add a parenthetical trainer narration line at Step 5 so the 'point is the map' teaching frame is spoken, not just held in the trainer's head. Without it, the step risks reading as a repo tour. Suggested form: '(Say: "Everything the workbook needs — source, render, output, deployment — lives in the same system.")'

Step 11 closing line ('close by naming the loop') gives the trainer no exact wording. The crystallisation beat depends on the trainer landing the phrasing well. Add the canonical sentence as a trainer line: e.g. '(Say: "Rules, inspect, plan, edit, check, build, compound. That loop is the difference between asking Claude for help and working inside an agentic system.")'. Ambiguous instructions at Steps 1, 4, 6, and 10 share the same root: the script states teaching points for the trainer's benefit but does not mark which phrases must be spoken aloud.
